paragraph: The single-family market in Fort Lee is sparse, with only 22 homes sold over six months at a median of $1,116,900. Sellers are okay with waiting for the right buyer rather than pricing competitively to sell extremely fast, even though 54 percent of homes sold above asking and the sale-to-list ratio sits at 1.02. The median days on market of 39.5 suggests patience on both sides. Multi-family properties are even rarer, with 6 sales at a median of $1,467,500 and a notably lower sale-to-list ratio of 0.99, indicating less upward pressure. By contrast, condos and townhouses are the real volume of Fort Lee with 142 sales over six months at a median of $589,500. This category includes new-construction side-by-side duplexes built on torn-down single-family lots, not high-rise units alone. Buildings like the Buckingham, The Palisades, and Mediterranean South are moving units consistently, though only 22 percent of sales cleared asking price and median days on market sits at 28.5, suggesting buyers have some room to negotiate. Co-ops sit at the entry point with 89 sales at a median of $240,000, but they move slower at 53 median days on market. Horizon House, The Plaza, and Mediterranean Towers North and West are buildings to know, and they come with board approval, financing restrictions, and resale friction that not every buyer is aware of going in. If you're comparing tiers, the deciding factor isn't market heat. It's whether you want the space and grounds of a single-family, the low-maintenance lifestyle of a condo, or the lower price point of a co-op.
